w:r.w:t: The global prevalence of diabetes, is rising rapidly, driven largely by excess body weight.
Despite major advances in understanding metabolic regulation, current obesity treatments often have limited success or side effects.
billion adults worldwide hence, necessitating more innovative therapeutic approaches.
The Limitations of Current Treatment Approaches
Single-target medications often provide insufficient glycaemic control and modest weight loss. Patients frequently require multiple medications, increasing complexity and reducing adherence.
cause weight gain or are weightneutral, failing to address the coloured obesity component of the disease.
These limitations highlight the urgent need for more effective, comprehensive treatment strategies that can simultaneously address multiple aspects of metabolic dysfunction.
A Summary Of Hormonal Changes Following
involves rerouting of the digestive tract, via creation of the
to bypass much of the stomach and duodenum and directly feed nutrients into the jejunum. Food passes through the Roux limb
, while digestive juices continue to flow through the stomach to the duodenum
. Hormonal modulation is presented for pancreatic insulin and glucagon, duodenum-derived cholecystokinin
increases are indicated by green boxes, decreases are indicated by red boxes and no discernible change is indicated by

In the last decade bariatric surgery was the choice for obesity when we did not have much pharmacotherapy choice. So now in last few years we are having more developments and more potent drugs for obesity management so bariatric surgery choice is changing & pharmacotherapy will replace it
Brain Targeting Signal For Metabolic Control
Recent research has revealed complex interactions between the brain and body in regulating hunger and energy

The discovery of leptin, a hormone from fat tissue, initially raised hopes for treating obesity, as it promotes fullness and increases energy burn. However, its effects in obesity are found to be limited.

It is a Single molecule engineered to activate multiple receptor target, but each component of the molecule do not interact with each other & they act separately on their receptors & they maintain their individual identity & integrity.
Provides synergistic effects through coordinated receptor activation.
Simplifies treatment regimens and improves adherence.
action produces greater insulin secretion than the sum of their individual effects
affects pancreas, brain, adipose tissue, gastrointestinal tract, and liver simultaneously
This complementary action offers superior efficacy of dual-targeted therapy
alone, particularly for patients with both diabetes and obesity.
effect refers to the enhanced insulin secretion observed when glucose is administered orally
trial and subsequent analyses confirm robust weight reduction in non-diabetic adults. On average, 15% body weight reduction was achieved with 5
term data demonstrate durability of effect with continued therapy.
The most common adverse events are gastrointestinal—nausea, vomiting,
, constipation, and dyspepsia. These are dose-dependent and most prominent during escalation. Higher doses
are associated with increased discontinuation rates due to intolerance. Serious contraindications include a personal or family history of medullary thyroid carcinoma and
Begin low and titrate slowly to
Do not escalate doses more frequently than every 4
Monitor adherence and tolerance; weight loss of 15
tolerance, hyperinsulinemia, hepatic steatosis, and body weight.
Preclinical data show marked Weight loss, improved
Fig 7: Key physiological effects of glucagon on the brain, heart, liver, GI tract, and pancreas.

Head-to-head trials consistently demonstrate superior efficacy of dual agonists compared to single-target therapies, with incrementally greater benefits in both glycaemic control and weight reduction.
Estrogen has been found to demonstrates leptin-like effects at the hypothalamus and protects pancreatic islets from damage associated with metabolic disease, thus coinciding with the pharmacology of

plays a crucial role in glucose and lipid metabolism, impacting insulin sensitivity, glucose uptake, and fat oxidation.

s influence on metabolic processes makes it a potential therapeutic target for conditions like obesity, type 2
has been shown to have cardioprotective effects, including reducing atherosclerosis and improving lipid profiles.
can be induced in muscle under stress, helping to alleviate mitochondrial dysfunction and endoplasmic reticulum stress.
is mainly produced by the liver, and it plays a role in regulating fatty acid oxidation and glucose homeostasis.
influences glucose metabolism in adipose tissue and can promote the
of white adipose tissue, which can be beneficial for energy expenditure.
acts in the brain to regulate light
dark cycle activity and potentially influence feeding
has an anti-inflammatory role in the pancreas, potentially impacting conditions like pancreatitis.
s effects on glucose and lipid metabolism make it a promising candidate for treating obesity and type 2

s ability to improve lipid profiles and reduce inflammation makes it relevant to cardiovascular health.

as a single-drug formulation in Type 1
diabetes patients further point to the importance of activating multiple signals.

Multi-mechanism antibiotics and antivirals to combat resistance and enhance efficacy.
Combining bronchodilatory, anti-inflammatory, and mucolytic properties for conditions like
Combining antihypertensive, antiplatelet, and lipid-lowering activities in a single molecule for comprehensive
Targeting multiple cancer pathways simultaneously to overcome resistance mechanisms and enhance therapeutic efficacy.
Addressing insulin resistance, dyslipidemia, and obesity with unified therapeutic approaches.
Metabolic disease treatment is shifting from symptom management to Comprehensive Metabolic Control.
Like Efficacy with Medications and a Expanded Therapeutic Impact.
Numerous promising therapies are in the clinical pipelines have been poised for regulatory review.
Our field is entering a new era of effective, convenient,
metabolic care, Less Burden, Improved Adherence and Ongoing Innovation.

Unimolecular polypharmacy represents a transformative approach in managing metabolic disorders, combining multiple receptor targets into a single molecule to enhance efficacy, simplify treatment, and improve adherence. Dual and
, glucagon, amylin, and steroid-based combinations, demonstrate superior
control, weight reduction, and multi-organ benefits
traditional therapies. These innovations signal a shift toward comprehensive, patient-
metabolic care, offering bariatric surgery–like outcomes with lower treatment burden and broader therapeutic impact.
